Quick Start

Choose your stack below and follow the steps. Every quickstart follows the same flow:

PackageWhat you’ll doTime
CLI / Docker →brew install → first ping~2 min
Laravel →composer require → artisan install → first cron run~3 min
Node.js / TypeScript →npm install → npx tsx quickstart.ts~3 min
Python →pip install → python quickstart.py~3 min
Go →go get → go run quickstart.go~3 min
Rust →cargo add → cargo run~3 min
PHP →composer require → php quickstart.php~3 min
Ruby →gem install → ruby quickstart.rb~3 min
Ruby on Rails →bundle install → auto-instrument~3 min
.NET →dotnet add package → dotnet run~3 min

Before you start

  1. Sign up at app.crontinel.com (free tier included).
  2. Create an app from the dashboard — give it a name (e.g. “my-first-app”).
  3. Copy your API key (crn_live_...) from the app settings page.

That’s it — on to your package.

Verify it works

After following your package’s quickstart:

  1. Go to app.crontinel.com → Apps → select your app
  2. Check the Cron, Queue, or Events sections — data should appear within 30 seconds.
  3. If you see your job run with exit codes and duration, you’re all set.

No data yet? Make sure CRONTINEL_API_KEY is set in the environment where your code runs, and that your app can reach https://app.crontinel.com (no firewall blocking).

Next steps

  • Set up alerts to get notified when a job fails
  • Learn about monitors for deeper insight
  • Connect your self-hosted instance to the SaaS
